original_user_request: Create a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js from CDN
precise_rewrite: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game that runs entirely within a single HTML file, utilizing Three.js library sourced via CDN for rendering.
assumptions: The user desires a fully self-contained solution without external dependencies beyond the CDN-hosted Three.js library.

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing between development speed and ensuring a high-quality game experience.

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize realism in the 3D environment or creating an engaging, fun gameplay experience.

speed.rationale: The user likely wants a quick solution to start working on the game.

quality.rationale: While speed is important, maintaining a good quality of the game is crucial for user satisfaction.

realism.rationale: Realism is less critical compared to fun and quality in this context.

fun.rationale: The game should be enjoyable, which often requires a balance of gameplay mechanics and visual appeal.

security.rationale: Using CDN-hosted libraries is generally safe, but the user may have specific security concerns that need addressing.

cost.rationale: The solution should be cost-effective, leveraging free resources like CDN-hosted libraries.

definition_of_done: A single HTML file containing the complete game.
The game uses Three.js for rendering.
The game is playable and demonstrates basic Crossy Road mechanics.
The solution is self-contained with no external dependencies beyond CDN-hosted libraries.
conflict_rules: In case of a conflict between speed and quality, prioritize quality to ensure the game remains enjoyable and well-crafted.
